title: elymus trachycaulus subsp. sierrae
intro: Elymus trachycaulus subsp. sierrae, commonly known as Sierra wildrye, is a type of grass native to the western mountain regions of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic, due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids in its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Elymus trachycaulus subsp. sierrae include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
